_metadata.source_authority: Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC) & GSTN
governing_rules: CGST Rule 48(4), (5), (6) — Manner of Issuing Invoice
Notification No. 01/2022-Central Tax dated 24-02-2022 (Rs. 20 Cr threshold)
Notification No. 10/2023-Central Tax dated 26-05-2023 (Rs. 5 Cr threshold, effective 01-08-2023)
GSTN Advisory dated 05-11-2024 (30-day IRP reporting limit, effective 01-04-2025)
ICAI Handbook on Invoicing under GST, June 2025 edition

rules.title: E-Invoice Mandate — Applicability and Threshold
rules.description: Registered persons whose Aggregate Annual Turnover (AATO) in any preceding financial year from 2017-18 onwards exceeds the notified threshold must prepare invoices by uploading to the Invoice Registration Portal (IRP) and obtaining an IRN.
rules.aato_basis: AATO is computed on PAN-basis — aggregate turnover of ALL GSTINs under a single PAN across all States/UTs in India in any preceding financial year from 2017-18
rules.perpetual_application: Once a taxpayer's AATO exceeds the threshold in any preceding year, e-invoicing applies in all subsequent years even if AATO later falls below the threshold
applicable_transactions: Credit notes and debit notes issued by notified persons against B2B/export invoices
not_applicable_transactions: Nil-rated or fully exempt supplies (no tax invoice required)
Receipt vouchers, payment vouchers, refund vouchers

rules.title: Invalid Invoice Without IRN from Notified Taxpayer
rules.description: An invoice issued by a notified person (to whom Rule 48(4) applies) in any manner other than uploading to IRP and obtaining an IRN is NOT treated as a valid invoice under Rule 48(5).
consequences: Invoice without valid IRN is legally invalid under CGST Rules
Recipient CANNOT claim ITC on an invoice without valid IRN from a notified supplier
Goods transported on the basis of such invoice may be detained (e-way bill cannot be generated without IRN for notified persons)
Supplier is liable to penalty under Section 122 of CGST Act
Commissioner may direct the registrant to generate IRN retrospectively only under specific circumstances

rules.title: Entities Exempt from E-Invoice Requirement
rules.description: Certain categories of registered persons are exempt from the e-invoice mandate even where their AATO exceeds the threshold.

exempt_categories.entity: Suppliers of services by way of admission to cinematograph films on multiplex screens

exempt_categories.basis: Notification 13/2020-CT — SEZ Developers are NOT exempt

rules.important_note: Exemption is entity-wide — an exempt entity does not generate e-invoices for any of its supplies. The declaration under Rule 46(r) must appear on invoices issued by such taxpayers where their AATO exceeds the notified threshold.

rules.title: 30-Day Time Limit for Reporting E-Invoices on IRP
rules.description: As per GSTN Advisory dated 05-11-2024 (effective 01-04-2025), taxpayers with AATO of Rs. 10 crore and above must report e-invoices on the IRP within 30 days from the invoice date.
conditions: Applies to: taxpayers with AATO >= Rs. 10 Crore — effective 01-04-2025
The 30-day limit applies to: Tax Invoices and Credit/Debit Notes reported under e-invoicing
The IRP portal will reject invoice uploads beyond the 30-day window
Invoices rejected by IRP for being late are non-compliant; ITC not available to recipient
Previous (01-11-2023 to 31-03-2025): 30-day limit applied to AATO >= Rs. 100 Crore
For AATO between Rs. 5 Crore and Rs. 10 Crore: no defined time limit under current advisory (as of March 2026)
Correction mechanism: if invoice missed the window, taxpayer must report it as an amendment in GSTR-1 (cannot obtain IRN for such invoices)
rules.applicable_to: Registered taxpayers with AATO >= Rs. 10 Crore (from 01-04-2025)
rules.amendment_ref: GSTN Advisory dated 05-11-2024; earlier circular for Rs. 100 Crore threshold dated 06-09-2023

rules.title: E-Invoice Schema — Document Details (Mandatory)
rules.description: The mandatory document-level fields in FORM GST INV-01 JSON schema for reporting to IRP.

fields: SupTyp (Supply Type): B2B, B2C, SEZWP (SEZ with payment), SEZWOP (SEZ without payment), EXPWP (Export with payment), EXPWOP (Export without payment), DEXP (Deemed Export)
Typ (Document Type): INV (Invoice), CRN (Credit Note), DBN (Debit Note)
No (Document Number: max 16 characters per Rule 46)

rules.title: E-Invoice Schema — Seller and Buyer Details (Mandatory)
rules.description: Mandatory party-level details in the e-invoice JSON schema.
seller_mandatory_fields: GSTIN (Seller's GSTIN — 15-digit, must be active and valid)
LglNm (Legal Name of Seller — max 100 characters)
Stcd (State Code — 2-digit code per GST state code list)
buyer_mandatory_fields: GSTIN (Buyer's GSTIN — mandatory for B2B; 'URP' for unregistered; 'NA' for export)
LglNm (Legal Name of Buyer — max 100 characters)

rules.title: E-Invoice Schema — Line Item Details (Mandatory)
rules.description: Mandatory line-item level details in the e-invoice JSON schema. At least one line item is mandatory.
mandatory_item_fields: HsnCd (HSN/SAC Code — mandatory, minimum digits as per notification based on AATO)
Qty (Quantity — numeric with 3 decimal places; optional for services)
Unit (Unit Code — from list: NOS, KGS, MTR, LTR etc.; NA for services)
UnitPrice (Unit Price before discount — up to 18 decimal places)
AssAmt (Assessable Value = TotAmt - Discount — must be > 0)
IgstAmt OR (CgstAmt + SgstAmt) — one must be populated based on supply type
TotItemVal (Total Item Value = AssAmt + IgstAmt or CgstAmt + SgstAmt + CesAmt)
rules.totitemval_note: From 01-02-2026: TotItemVal validation relaxed for RSP-based calculations — items sold at MRP/RSP may have minor discrepancies in TotItemVal due to rounding. Per Notification No. 20/2025-CT dated 31-12-2025.

rules.title: E-Invoice Schema — Invoice Value Details (Mandatory)
rules.description: Mandatory invoice-level total value details in the e-invoice JSON schema.
mandatory_value_fields: AssVal (Total Assessable Value — sum of all line item AssAmt)
IgstAmt (Total IGST Amount — for inter-State or export transactions)
CgstAmt (Total CGST Amount — for intra-State transactions)
SgstAmt (Total SGST/UTGST Amount — for intra-State transactions)
CesNonAdvolAmt (Cess on specific items not calculated as percentage, if applicable)
StCesAmt (State Cess — if applicable in some states)
TotInvVal (Total Invoice Value = AssVal + IgstAmt or CgstAmt + SgstAmt + CesAmt - Discount + RndOffAmt)
rules.validation: IRP validates that TotInvVal matches the sum of all component amounts within prescribed tolerance

rules.title: E-Invoice Schema — Optional Sections
rules.description: Optional sections in the e-invoice JSON schema that may be required for specific transaction types.

optional_sections.when_needed: When payment terms need to be communicated to recipient

fields: DocPerdDtls (Document Period — from and to dates)
PrecDocDtls (Preceding document reference — for credit/debit notes: original invoice number and date)
optional_sections.when_needed: Credit notes and debit notes MUST include PrecDocDtls with original invoice number and date
For all export transactions (EXPWP or EXPWOP supply type)
Can be included in e-invoice for automatic e-way bill generation. E-way bill auto-generated on IRP if TransId or VehNo is provided and consignment value exceeds Rs. 50,000

rules.title: IRN Generation — Technical Process and Hash Algorithm
rules.description: The Invoice Reference Number (IRN) is a unique 64-character alphanumeric reference generated by the IRP using the SHA-256 hash algorithm.

input_parameters: Document Number (Invoice/Credit/Debit Note number — exactly as on invoice)
Document Type: INV, CRN, or DBN
irn_generation.output: 64-character alphanumeric hash — unique for each combination of the 4 inputs above
irn_generation.uniqueness_guarantee: Same supplier GSTIN + document number + FY + document type combination cannot generate a second IRN — duplicate IRN generation is blocked by IRP

rules.irn_validity: An IRN is valid permanently — there is no expiry on an issued IRN. However, a cancelled IRN cannot be used for any purpose.
rules.irp_response: IRP returns: signed JSON (with IRP digital signature), IRN, QR code, acknowledgement number, and acknowledgement date

rules.title: QR Code on E-Invoice — Mandatory Content
rules.description: The QR code embedded in e-invoices (generated by IRP) must contain at minimum the following information. QR codes for B2C invoices are different (seller-generated, not IRP-generated).
qr_code_content_b2b_einvoice: Main HSN code (HSN code of the item with highest value)

qr_code_b2c_rule.applicability: Suppliers with AATO > Rs. 500 Crore must have a dynamic QR code on B2C invoices (not through IRP) — per Notification No. 14/2020-CT and related circulars
qr_code_b2c_rule.content: UPI link or payment QR code enabling recipient to make payment
qr_code_b2c_rule.exemption: Insurance companies, banking companies, NBFCs, GTA, passenger transport — exempt from B2C QR code requirement

rules.verification: Recipients can verify the IRN and QR code at https://einvoice1.gst.gov.in/Others/verifysignedeinvoice

rules.title: IRP Validation Checks Before Issuing IRN
rules.description: The Invoice Registration Portal performs the following validation checks before issuing an IRN.

validation_checks.rule: Supplier GSTIN must be valid, active, and not cancelled. GSTIN must match the logged-in taxpayer's GSTIN.
Buyer GSTIN must be valid and active for B2B transactions. For export: 'NA' is accepted. For unregistered: 'URP' is accepted.
Same combination of Supplier GSTIN + Invoice Number + Financial Year + Document Type cannot be submitted twice. IRP will reject with 'IRN already generated' error.

Invoice date cannot be before the e-invoice applicability date for that taxpayer. Invoice date cannot be future-dated. Invoice date must be within current or immediately preceding financial year for IRN generation.
For taxpayers with AATO >= Rs. 10 Crore: invoice date cannot be more than 30 days before date of IRP upload. IRP rejects invoices older than 30 days.
HSN/SAC code must be a valid code from the GST HSN master maintained by GSTN. Invalid HSN codes will be rejected.
Invoice number must not exceed 16 characters and must only contain alphanumerics, hyphens, and slashes as per Rule 46(b).
IRP validates that tax amounts (IGST or CGST+SGST) are consistent with the declared assessable value and tax rate. Discrepancies beyond tolerance trigger rejection.
TotItemVal at line item level must match AssAmt + tax amounts. From 01-02-2026: tolerance allowed for RSP/MRP-based items under Notification No. 20/2025-CT.
Place of Supply state code must be consistent with supply type (inter-State = IGST applicable; intra-State = CGST+SGST applicable).
IRP verifies that the supplier is a notified taxpayer who is required to generate e-invoices. Non-notified taxpayers cannot generate IRNs.

rules.title: E-Invoice Cancellation — Rules and Process
rules.description: An IRN once generated can be cancelled within 24 hours on the IRP. After 24 hours, amendment is required through GSTR-1.
cancellation_rules: Cancellation within 24 hours of IRN generation: allowed directly on IRP portal (or through API/GSP)
Cancellation after 24 hours: not permitted on IRP. Taxpayer must file an amendment in GSTR-1 (Table 9A or 9B as applicable)
A cancelled IRN cannot be reused
After cancellation, a fresh invoice must be raised with a DIFFERENT invoice number — same invoice number cannot be reused
Partial cancellation is NOT permitted — the entire e-invoice must be cancelled
Goods already in transit on e-way bill: e-way bill must also be cancelled before the e-invoice can be cancelled
Cancellation on IRP is final — it cannot be reversed
rules.amendment_in_gstr1: Where IRP-based cancellation is not possible (after 24 hours), the incorrect invoice is amended in GSTR-1. The corrected details flow to the buyer's GSTR-2B in the month the amendment is filed.

rules.rule_code: CBIC Rule 48(4) — E-Invoice for Credit/Debit Notes
rules.title: E-Invoice Requirement for Credit Notes and Debit Notes
rules.description: Notified taxpayers must generate IRN for Credit Notes (CRN) and Debit Notes (DBN) issued against B2B/export invoices, just as they do for invoices.
conditions: CDNs (Credit Notes and Debit Notes) issued by notified taxpayers against B2B invoices must be reported to IRP
Document Type in FORM GST INV-01: CRN for Credit Notes; DBN for Debit Notes
Original invoice reference (PrecDocDtls) is mandatory in the e-CDN
IRN is generated for each CDN just as for invoices
Same 30-day reporting window applies for CDNs (for AATO >= Rs. 10 Crore from 01-04-2025)
CDNs issued for B2C transactions do not require IRN
CDNs for exempt supplies or bill-of-supply transactions do not require IRN

rules.rule_code: CBIC Rule 48(4) — Integration with GSTR-1 and E-Way Bill
rules.title: Auto-Population of GSTR-1 and E-Way Bill from E-Invoice
rules.description: E-invoices (IRN-generated) are automatically reflected in GSTR-1 and GSTR-2B, and e-way bills can be auto-generated from the e-invoice data.
gstr1_integration: E-invoice data is auto-populated in GSTR-1 of the supplier in the relevant table (B2B, B2CL, Export, Credit Notes, etc.)
Auto-populated data in GSTR-1 can be edited or deleted by the supplier before filing
Buyer receives auto-populated data in their GSTR-2B based on the supplier's e-invoice/GSTR-1 data
E-invoices uploaded after GSTR-1 filing date for that month flow into the next month's GSTR-1
eway_bill_integration: When transporter details (TransId or VehNo and Distance) are included in the e-invoice JSON, IRP automatically generates an e-way bill
No separate e-way bill generation required on NIC e-way bill portal if auto-generated through e-invoice
E-way bill number (EwbNo) is returned along with the IRN in the IRP response
If transport details are NOT included in e-invoice: e-way bill must be separately generated on NIC e-way bill portal (ewaybillgst.gov.in) using the IRN

rules.title: Self-Invoices Under RCM — Not Covered by E-Invoice Mandate
rules.description: Self-invoices issued by recipients under the Reverse Charge Mechanism (Section 31(3)(f) read with Rule 47A) are NOT required to have an IRN.
conditions: Self-invoices issued by registered recipients for purchases from unregistered suppliers under RCM (Section 9(3) or 9(4)) do NOT require an IRN from IRP
The e-invoice mandate under Rule 48(4) applies to invoices issued by the SUPPLIER — not to self-invoices issued by the recipient
Self-invoices follow the format of Rule 46 but are not uploaded to IRP
This is clarified in FAQs published on einvoice1.gst.gov.in

rules.title: QR Code on B2C Invoices — Separate Obligation for Large Taxpayers
rules.description: Suppliers with AATO > Rs. 500 Crore must display a dynamic QR code on B2C invoices enabling payment to the supplier. This is separate from the IRP-generated e-invoice QR code.
conditions: Applicability: AATO > Rs. 500 Crore — per Notification No. 14/2020-CT dated 21-03-2020
Effective date: 01-12-2020 (with various compliance relaxations initially)
QR code must encode a link to the UPI or any other payment mode approved by RBI
QR code must be 'dynamic' — unique per transaction, encoding at minimum: payee VPA (UPI ID), payee name, amount, invoice reference
Applicable even to B2C invoices that do not require IRP-generated IRN
Exemptions from B2C QR code: banking companies, insurance companies, NBFCs, GTA, passenger transport, multiplex — same entities exempt from e-invoicing

rules.title: E-Invoice for Supplies to SEZ Units and SEZ Developers
rules.description: Supplies to SEZ units or SEZ developers for authorised operations are covered under the e-invoice mandate for notified taxpayers.
conditions: Notified taxpayers must generate IRN for supplies to SEZ units (supply type: SEZWP or SEZWOP)
SEZWP = SEZ with payment of IGST; SEZWOP = SEZ without payment of IGST (under LUT/Bond)
SEZ Developers: not exempt from e-invoicing — they must generate IRN for their supplies
SEZ Units: ARE exempt from e-invoicing for their own supplies to DTA/other buyers
GSTIN of the SEZ unit (buyer) is mandatory in the e-invoice
IRN and QR code must be printed on the physical/electronic invoice sent to SEZ unit

rules.title: Record Retention for E-Invoices
rules.description: E-invoice data and IRN details must be retained for the period prescribed under Section 36 of CGST Act and Companies Act 2013.
conditions: E-invoice signed JSON (IRP-signed response) must be retained — not just the PDF printout
Retention period: 72 months (6 years) from due date of Annual Return under Section 36; for companies: 8 years under Companies Act 2013 Section 128(5) — whichever is longer
IRP does not permanently store e-invoices — taxpayer is responsible for their own retention
IRP retains e-invoice data for only 24 hours (after which cancellation is not possible on IRP, and invoice data is no longer retrievable from IRP directly)
Taxpayer ERP/billing system must store the complete IRN, signed JSON, and QR code image
On audit or investigation: taxpayer must produce the IRP-signed JSON as evidence of valid e-invoice

rules.title: E-Invoice API — Integration Methods for Taxpayers
rules.description: Taxpayers can generate e-invoices through multiple channels: direct API integration, through GSPs, offline tools, or the government-provided web portal.

integration_channels.description: Taxpayer ERP integrates directly with IRP API using registered API credentials. Supports high-volume batch generation. Recommended for large enterprises.
integration_channels.gsp: GST Suvidha Providers (GSPs) — authorized service providers who can connect to IRP API on behalf of taxpayers

integration_channels.description: Provided by NIC — for small/medium taxpayers generating low volume of invoices. Offline data entry in Excel, bulk upload to IRP.
Manual entry on the IRP portal — for very low volume taxpayers. Not suitable for real-time generation.
For smallest taxpayers — not widely adopted

rules.api_authentication: API credentials issued by NIC; GSTIN-specific. ERP must send digitally signed JSON payload in FORM GST INV-01 format.

rules.title: Clarifications on E-Invoice and QR Code — Circular 211/5/2024
rules.description: Circular No. 211/5/2024-GST dated 26-06-2024 provides clarifications on several e-invoice and QR code related issues.
key_clarifications: IRN can be generated on the original invoice even if it was initially not reported to IRP — within the applicable time window. After the time window, GSTR-1 amendment is the only recourse.

It is not mandatory to print the IRP-signed QR code on the physical invoice if the QR code generated from the seller's ERP system encodes the same mandatory fields including IRN. However, the IRP-signed JSON must be retained.
For invoices with multiple line items: the QR code encodes the top-level invoice details only (IRN, total value, main HSN), not individual line items
E-invoice requirement for credit notes: a credit note issued by a notified taxpayer against a B2B invoice is required to have an IRN even if the credit note brings the net taxable value to zero
E-invoice system downtime: if IRP is unavailable, invoice can be issued and IRN obtained later within the applicable time window — this is not a penalty-attracting situation

rules.rule_code: CBIC — Penalties for Non-Compliance with E-Invoicing
rules.title: Penalties for Non-Compliance with E-Invoice Rules
rules.description: Non-compliance with e-invoice requirements leads to the following legal consequences.

penalty_provisions.consequence: Invoice without IRN from notified taxpayer is invalid — treated as if no invoice was issued
Penalty for issuing incorrect invoice or not issuing invoice: Rs. 10,000 per invoice or tax evaded/ITC improperly claimed, whichever is higher

penalty_provisions.provision: Section 16(2) of CGST Act (recipient ITC denial)
penalty_provisions.consequence: Recipient's ITC denied on invoices without valid IRN from notified supplier. Recipient liable for ITC reversal with interest.
From 01-04-2020: notified taxpayers cannot generate e-way bills using their own invoice reference — e-way bill must reference the IRN. If no IRN, goods cannot move with a valid e-way bill.
Non-generation of e-invoices may be flagged during GST audit (Section 65/66) or assessment proceedings, leading to demand of tax along with interest and penalty.
